title: Utility poles still obstruct sections of the Nagdhunga-Mugling widening project
summary: A June 2026 Kathmandu Post report, citing project and electricity-authority officials, said 75 poles remained on the eastern section and 107 on the western section of the 94.66 km corridor. This dossier records the published claim for public follow-up; it does not assert that the counts are unchanged today.

provenance.sourceTitle: Utility pole relocation delays hobble road widening projects

title: Central Kathmandu drainage capacity remains a monsoon follow-up issue
summary: Kathmandu Metropolitan City's public report said roads around Ganatantra Park, Jai Nepal Hall and Jamal were inundated during 2026 rains, and identified limited drainage capacity plus debris carried by runoff as contributing factors. This is an official-source dossier, not a firsthand field report.

provenance.sourceTitle: Municipal response after a Samakhushi river wall collapse reduced risk to eight homes

title: Bancharedanda landfill service life is under documented pressure
summary: Kathmandu Metropolitan City reported that waste volumes are shortening the planned service life of Bancharedanda and described source segregation, material-recovery centres and inter-municipal financing as needed responses. The record tracks the official statement and its follow-up window.

provenance.sourceTitle: Measures to extend landfill life through source reduction, material recovery and financial partnership

title: Dhangadhi and nearby Kailari settlements face recurring groundwater shortages
summary: A June 2026 Kathmandu Post report said Dhangadhi was distributing water by tanker in affected wards and cited municipal and WASH officials describing dry hand pumps, deeper borewells and weak groundwater recharge in nearby Kailari. The current field status still requires rechecking.

provenance.sourceTitle: Hand pumps are dry, even deep borewells no longer provide enough water
